title: Respond to a leaked credential
description: An incident procedure that rotates first, then scrubs and verifies, treating the leak as a security event.
body: Use when a live secret is discovered in source, history, logs, or a third party. Do not start by deleting the file. Steps: revoke the exposed credential at its issuer immediately; issue and deploy a replacement; remove the exposure from current code and scrub it from history; rescan to confirm; record the incident. Validation signals include the old credential being rejected and a clean rescan. Risk boundary: this is a security incident requiring human notification, so escalate to the secret's owner and never treat removal alone as resolution.
